description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-ca5cbafb is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate paren
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: duplicate of ddx-ca5cbafb / decomposition artifact
title: ddx work: codex provider subprocesses orphan to PID 1 when worker dies,: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.

acceptance:
1. Child bead for root SIGINT/SIGTERM context and user-facing shutdown message exists and is closed.
2. Child bead for interrupted attempt cleanup, unclaim, liveness cleanup, and regression tests exists and is closed.
3. `ddx work` and `ddx try` both print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` on first Ctrl-C/SIGTERM.
4. Interrupted non-terminal beads are open/re-claimable after cleanup and are not parked behind `work-retry-after`.
5. Second-signal hard-abort behavior is documented and tested or explicitly documented as best-effort.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestSignal|TestInterrupt|TestCancel|TestShutdown|TestDisrupted|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators routinely stop long-running `ddx work` / `ddx try` with Ctrl-C. The governing specs now require the first SIGINT/SIGTERM to cancel work cooperatively, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, preserve available evidence, release non-terminal claims, and leave unfinished beads re-claimable. The implementation beads need to land as one coherent interrupt story.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls cmd.Execute, and cli/cmd/root.go:31 calls rootCmd.Execute without a signal-backed context, so terminal SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the process instead of driving cooperative cancellation.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 documents signal as a stop condition, but there is no root signal handler that turns Ctrl-C into that condition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 can classify context cancellation as disrupted, but normal Ctrl-C does not currently reach this path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 now define the intended first-signal behavior and user-facing message.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate two implementation slices: root CLI signal context/user messaging, and work/try attempt cleanup/reclaim tests. When both children close, verify `ddx work` and `ddx try` behave consistently under Ctrl-C.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement resumable worktrees by default. Interrupted work may be preserved as evidence, but the safe default is cleanup and retry from scratch.
- Do not change queue ordering, routing policy, review policy, or cost-cap policy.
- Do not remove legacy work/execute-bead compatibility commands.
notes:
RELATED QUEUE ALIGNMENT
- ddx-9228a484 (StopCondition enum) now depends on this epic so Signal semantics are implemented after first-signal cooperative cancellation is defined.
- ddx-9f20a4bd (external claim liveness) intersects with interrupted heartbeat/liveness cleanup; do not reintroduce beads.jsonl heartbeat writes while implementing the cleanup child.
- ddx-eccc6efb (server-spawn ddx work subprocess) should rely on the same root signal behavior once server workers exec ddx work; keep server-specific SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation as a supervisor layer, not a second queue-drain implementation.
title: work: graceful interrupt shutdown for work and try

- TestModeMatrix_BeadExecution
- TestModeMatrix_TwoClonesShareXDG
- TestModeMatrix_OperatorExpectationDocumented
Use deterministic project roots, isolated XDG tempdirs, and a shared helper that makes the only allowed difference the on-disk anchor.

NON-SCOPE
Plugin/persona/meta-prompt/auto-commit/docs scenarios.

description:
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

description:
PROBLEM
Stop currently performs ad hoc cleanup inside WorkerManager.Stop, releasing the claimed bead and then killing only the recorded pid when pid > 0. That path does not prove that a managed worker tree with fake claude/codex descendants is fully terminated, and the cleanup logic is not centralized for reuse by other lifecycle paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1312 contains Stop, including the current pid-gated terminateProcessGroup call.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234-1286 computes pid and only escalates when pid > 0, so cleanup is limited to the recorded leader process.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1298-1312 finalizes the worker after the ad hoc kill path, which makes idempotence depend on repeated copies of the same logic rather than a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a shared managed-worker tree cleanup helper that sends SIGTERM to the managed process group, waits WatchdogKillGrace, then SIGKILLs any remaining descendants.
- Make Stop call that helper for server-managed workers before terminalizing the record.
- Preserve the bead.stopped event, claim release, and terminal status writes so repeated Stop calls remain idempotent.
- Add fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children so the test can prove the full descendant tree is gone after Stop.
- Add a second Stop call assertion so the test proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change watchdog reaping in this bead.
- Do not implement server shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or interactive sessions.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent" passes.
4. make install is run after the code change lands.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-ca5cbafb is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate paren
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: duplicate of ddx-ca5cbafb / decomposition artifact
title: ddx work: codex provider subprocesses orphan to PID 1 when worker dies,: part 1

acceptance: 1. A committed design doc exists describing the leftover-worktree-keyed reaping mechanism and why the cmdline-allowlist approach is insufficient. 2. At least one child build bead is created (ddx bead create) with command-based ACs and wired via ddx bead dep add <child> <this-epic>. 3. ddx bead dep tree <this-epic> shows the children.
description:
PROBLEM
Worker runs leave zombie/orphan helper processes (observed: orphan 'find' processes 23+ days old, plus orphan harness procs) that the operator had to hunt and kill manually ('kill the zombie claudes', 'get rid of the zombies and prevent them from happening').

WHY THE NAIVE FIX FAILS (from adversarial review)
- cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go acts only on processes where proc.PPID==1 AND looksLikeHarnessProcess(cmd) matches a fixed harness allowlist (claude|codex|gemini|opencode|pi) AND InferBead can attribute a bead+worktree from the cmdline.
- The harness is started with Setpgid (own process group) and the reaper does killGroup(proc.PID), so a 'find' still inside the harness group is already reaped when the harness dies.
- A 'find' that ESCAPED the group is not PPID-attributable to a harness and a bare 'find' cmdline cannot be mapped to a bead, so it is UNREACHABLE by the current reaper model. Extending looksLikeHarnessProcess to match 'find' would NOT fire.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (design, not implementation)
- A short design doc (under docs/dev/ or docs/helix/) specifying a leftover-PID/leftover-process scan keyed off the LEASE WORKTREE (e.g. processes with cwd or open files under a known execute-bead worktree root that outlive the lease), rather than a cmdline allowlist. Cover: detection signal, safety (never kill a live, legitimately-owned process), Linux-specificity, and how it composes with the existing group-kill + execution_cleanup_loop.go.
- Decompose into child build beads with command-based ACs and depend them on this epic.

NON-SCOPE (for THIS bead)
- No implementation in this bead — design + child beads only.
- Do not change Fizeau routing.
title: DESIGN: leak-proof helper-process reaping (leftover-PID scan)

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/persona/loader.go:16-53, cli/internal/persona/binding.go:17-53, and cli/internal/metaprompt/injector.go:11-101 each resolve project-local state, but there is no shared matrix proving that both modes resolve the same persona and prompt semantics.

ROOT CAUSE
Persona lookup, role binding, and CLAUDE.md prompt injection each handle project-scoped paths independently, so a mode regression would only show up in ad hoc feature tests.

PROPOSED FIX
Add TestModeMatrix_PersonaBinding and TestModeMatrix_MetaPromptSync. For Convention, the fixtures should use a per-test XDG project dir; for InTree, they should use the local .ddx anchor and ignore XDG. Assert role -> persona lookup and prompt sync return the same behavior modulo storage anchor.

NON-SCOPE
Registry lookup, worker drain, and auto-commit routing.
title: Add persona mode-matrix binding and meta-prompt sync coverage

acceptance: 1. A prompt-snapshot test (cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/...) shows .ddx/guardrails.md content present in the dispatched prompt when the file exists. 2. A test shows the assembled prompt is unchanged when the file is absent. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators re-paste hard per-project invariants ('do not delete anything from redpanda', 'never run perf tests on GitHub runners', 'do not touch the NAT') as prose every dispatch and every compaction, because there is no per-project guardrails file spliced into the load-bearing guardrail block the executor controls. Guardrails are currently static global consts.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (~1945-2065): guardrails are hardcoded const prompt strings (instrCoreConstraints, instrBeadOverride, the numbered load-bearing guardrails). No reader splices a per-project file. The only per-project surface (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) is harness-loaded prose, not part of the executor-controlled guardrail block.

PROPOSED FIX
- Read an OPTIONAL project-root file (.ddx/guardrails.md) and splice its contents into the execute-bead guardrail assembly so per-project invariants are injected on every dispatch (and therefore survive compaction automatically, since they are re-injected each iteration).
- Absent file = no behavioral change.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the existing static guardrail consts' wording.
- Do not add AGENTS.md parsing or change how the harness loads AGENTS.md.
- Do not implement per-bead overrides (this is per-project only).

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Inject per-project .ddx/guardrails.md into the execute-bead guardrail block

acceptance:
1. TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip passes against the GraphQL-backed implementation.
2. Backend conformance tests run against both JSONL and Axon for create/get/update/claim/deps/events/close behavior.
3. Any Axon-only limitation has an explicit skipped test or assertion message with a follow-up bead ID; no silent gaps remain.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Once GraphQL-backed operations exist, the backend needs parity coverage against JSONL so worker execution can trust AxonBackend without silently diverging on import/export, events, dependencies, and store behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go contains Axon-specific tests, but the parent acceptance requires JSONL import/export round trip and package-level conformance.
- cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go and related store tests exercise backend behavior primarily through JSONL paths.
- Without a parameterized conformance pass, AxonBackend can pass targeted CRUD tests but still diverge from RawBackend expectations.

PROPOSED FIX
- Parameterize the existing backend/store conformance suite so JSONL and Axon exercise the same core operations where practical.
- Add Axon import/export round-trip coverage.
- Document any intentional Axon-only limitation in test names and bead notes, not hidden in implementation comments.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new backend selection config plumbing.
- UI or server integration.
title: bead/axon: add Axon backend conformance and import/export coverage

description:
PROBLEM
Server.Shutdown only closes the bead hub and stops land coordinators. It does not stop the worker manager or sweep managed worker process trees, so server-owned descendants can outlive the server process when the server shuts down.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:269-283 has no worker-manager shutdown call at all; it only closes the bead hub and coordinator registry.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1323-1327 only stops the watchdog goroutine, so it does not clean live workers.
There is no manager-level shutdown entrypoint that iterates managed workers and stops them idempotently before the server returns.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a worker-manager shutdown method that stops all server-owned managed workers and waits for their terminalization.
- Call that method from Server.Shutdown before closing the hub and coordinators.
- Keep the operation idempotent so repeated shutdown calls do not double-transition beads or kill unrelated processes.
- Add TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees as the shutdown proof.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Stop/Reap semantics beyond reusing the shared helper.
- Do not implement UI controls or GraphQL mutations in this bead.
- Do not adopt external workers.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees proves server/manager shutdown cleans all server-owned worker process groups.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: stop managed workers during server shutdown

ROOT CAUSE
The resolver tests assert lookup order in isolation, while library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md:21-54 documents install topology without a cross-mode equivalence test.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the matrix suite with TestModeMatrix_PluginSkillResolution. Exercise a Convention fixture and an InTree fixture with the same plugin names, then assert project > global > baked-in precedence and the installed skill path resolves to plugin content without consulting the wrong storage anchor.

NON-SCOPE
Persona bindings, meta-prompt sync, and command auto-commit routing.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1133 ExecuteBeadWorker.Run is 2,473 LOC — pre-loop setup (:1133-:1499) plus the entire iteration loop (for { at :1500). The loop body has comment-delimited phases that are natural extraction seams (candidate selection :1500-1799, wedge-guard+atomic-claim :1840-1958, intake :2012, lint gate :2452, dispatch+watchdog :2559-2741, post-attempt classification :2742+).

PROPOSED FIX: STRICTLY MECHANICAL hoist (no behavior change): extract the loop body into (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runIteration(ctx, loopState, ...) (iterationOutcome, error), leaving Run as setup + for { runIteration(...) }. Introduce a small loopState/iterationOutcome struct for the values currently closed over. The phase helper functions mostly already exist further down the file.

NON-SCOPE: do NOT also extract the dispatch/post-attempt sub-phases here (separate follow-up); do NOT touch ExecuteBeadWithConfig; no behavior/event/ordering change.

acceptance:
1. TestBeadAndWorkPlanUseSameStoreInLinkedWorktree seeds conflicting bead states in a linked worktree and the canonical store, then proves bead show/list/export/ready and work plan report the same lifecycle state for the same bead.
2. TestWorkPlanDoesNotPreferStaleLocalSnapshotWhenCanonicalStoreExists covers the observed ddx-d94f1857 failure mode and proves a closed canonical bead is not returned as next claim from a stale local .ddx snapshot.
3. TestStaleWorktreeMasterDiagnosticReportsMissingExecuteBeadPath seeds worktrees.json with an old .execute-bead-wt master path and proves ddx doctor or an equivalent store diagnostic reports it.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/git/... -run 'TestBeadAndWorkPlanUseSameStoreInLinkedWorktree|TestWorkPlanDoesNotPreferStaleLocalSnapshotWhenCanonicalStoreExists|TestStaleWorktreeMasterDiagnosticReportsMissingExecuteBeadPath' passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
In a linked DDx worktree, `ddx bead show/export` reported ddx-d94f1857 as closed while `ddx work plan --json` still ranked the same bead as open and next claim. That can make a worker re-run already-completed work and breaks the queue as source of truth.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:286-320 resolves bead commands through beadWorkspaceRoot(), which calls git.FindNearestDDxWorkspace and can prefer a registered/master worktree. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:80-84 bypasses that path and opens bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot)), which prefers the local in-tree .ddx if present. In dogfood on 2026-06-13, the global worktree registry master pointed at an old execute-bead temp path, so bead commands and work plan observed different physical stores.

PROPOSED FIX
- Centralize queue store resolution for all bead/work/status commands behind one project-aware helper.
- Add a linked-worktree regression fixture where the local .ddx snapshot disagrees with the registered/master store and prove bead show, bead list, bead export, bead ready, work plan, and work status use the same store.
- Add a diagnostic when the worktree registry master points at a missing execute-bead path or a path outside the current repository family.
- Ensure the chosen store is visible in verbose/debug output so operators can identify split-brain state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl as the fix. Do not remove convention-root support. Do not change bead lifecycle semantics beyond consistent store selection.
title: work: unify bead store resolution across queue commands
